title: neomammillaria tetracantha
intro: Neomammillaria tetracantha, also known as the "cluster cactus," is a species of cactus in the genus Neomammillaria, characterized by its globular stems covered with sharp, yellowish-brown spines and vibrant pink flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have medicinal benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The extracts from Neomammillaria tetracantha have been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of allergies, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of Neomammillaria tetracantha can be found in the form of tinctures, teas, and infusions, although the efficacy and safety of these preparations require further scientific evaluation.
